# host_package_aur (Resource)

Manages a single AUR package through an AUR helper (`yay` or `paru`) and keeps its install reason marked as explicit. The helper runs as the invoking user and escalates through its own sudo calls.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "host_package_aur" "wl_kbptr" {
  name = "wl-kbptr"
}

# ignore_version defaults to true: presence is managed without planning
# rebuilds every time the AUR publishes a new version.
resource "host_package_aur" "claude_code" {
  name = "claude-code"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) AUR package name.

### Optional

- `autoremove` (Boolean) Remove unused dependencies recursively with `pacman -Rns` when this package is removed. Defaults to false.
- `ignore_version` (Boolean) Ignore available version updates. When true, the resource manages package presence and explicit install reason without planning upgrades, and skips AUR network lookups during refresh. Defaults to true.
- `version` (String) Package version policy. Only `latest` is currently supported. Used for upgrade planning only when `ignore_version` is false.

### Read-Only

- `candidate_version` (String) Latest package version reported by the AUR helper. Null when `ignore_version` is true.
- `id` (String) Resource identifier, equal to `name`.
- `installed_version` (String) Installed package version from the local pacman database.
